document.masterDocTitle: Taxonomic review of the tingine genera Cysteochila, Hurdchila, Physatocheila, and Xynotingis from Japan, with description of a new genus (Hemiptera: Heteroptera: Tingidae)

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: Taxonomic review of the tingine genera Cysteochila, Hurdchila, Physatocheila, and Xynotingis from Japan, with description of a new genus (Hemiptera: Heteroptera: Tingidae)

mods:name.mods:affiliation: Entomological Laboratory, Graduate School of Bioresource and Bioenvironmental Sciences, Kyushu University, Fukuoka, Japan. kodokusignal @ gmail. com; https: // orcid. org / 0000 - 0002 - 2238 - 5015 & Research Fellowship for Young Scientists (DC 1), Japan Society for the Promotion of Science, Tokyo, Japan
Laboratory of Entomology, Faculty of Agriculture, Tokyo University of Agriculture, Kanagawa, Japan.

heading.emphasis: Key to the genera and subgenera of Tingidae occurring in Japan

subSubSection.paragraph: Modified after the keys provided by Takeya (1962) and Yamada & Tomokuni (2012).

paragraph.keyLead:
1 Pronotum quinquecarinate.................................................
Cantacader Amyot & Serville, 1843

2 Pronotum with undeveloped posterior proccess...........................................
Phatnoma Fieber, 1844
- Pronotum with well-developed posterior proccess............................................................ 3

4 Cu (cubital) vein of hemelytron indistinct; R+M (radiomedial) vein indistinct.................
Agramma Stephens, 1829

- Cu vein of hemelytron distinct; R+M vein distinct..........................................
Eteoneus Distant, 1904
5 Paranotum expanding outward, not covering pronotal disc..................................................... 6
- Paranotum expanding inward, covering pronotal disc........................................................ 27
6 Paranotum carinate in its entire length; anterior margin of hemelytron nearly straight................................ 7
- Paranotum rounded at least in posterior part; anterior margin of hemelytron curved................................. 9

7 Head with four spines; paranotum without distinct areolae in its entire length; R+M vein of hemelytron not carinate...........................................................................................
Leptoypha Stål, 1873
- Head with five spines; paranotum with distinct areolae at least at level of calli; R+M vein of hemelytron carinate......... 8

8 Collar not reflexed upward; paranotum with distinct areolae in anterior half...............
Eritingis Drake & Ruhoff, 1962

- Collar reflexed upward; paranotum with distinct areolae at level of calli................
Perissonemia Drake & Poor, 1937

10 Discoidal area of hemelytron not expanding beyond middle of hemelytron.........................
Metasalis Lee, 1971
- Discoidal area of hemelytron expanding beyond middle of hemelytron.......................................... 11

11 Antennal segment III as wide as segment IV.............................................
Catoplatus Spinola, 1837

12 Antennal segment III wider than segment II..............................................
Copium Thunberg, 1822

14 Pronotum and hemelytron covered with pubescence longer than diameter of compound eye....................................................................................
Tingis ( Tropidocheila ) shaowuana Drake & Maa 1953

- Pronotum and hemelytron covered with pubescence shorter than diameter of compound eye or glabrous.........................................................
Tingis Fabricius, 1803
(nominotypical subgenus of
Tingis Fabricius, 1803
)

17 Outer margin of paranotum and anterior margin of hemelytron with denticles.....................
Corythucha Stål, 1873
- Outer margin of paranotum and anterior margin of hemelytron without denticles.................................. 18

18 Pronotum tricarinate; hood semi-ellipsoid..............................................
Derephysia Spinola, 1837

- Pronotum unicarinate; hood roof-shaped...........................................
Uhlerites latiorus Takeya, 1931

20 Head with two spines; median and occipital spines on head absent, collected from moss.............................................................................................................
Acalypta Westwood, 1840

- Head with four spines; median spine on head absent, collected from woody plants...........
Uhlerites debilis (Uhler, 1896)

22 Outer margin of paranotum with robust denticles in its entire length; anterior margin of hemelytron with robust denticles in its entire length; R+M vein of hemelytron with denticles in its entire length.......... Acanthomoplax Souma & Kamitani, 2020

- Outer margin of paranotum without robust denticles in its entire length; anterior margin of hemelytron without robust denticles in its entire length; R+M vein of hemelytron without denticles in its entire length.................................. 23

23 Paranotum carinate in anterior part and rounded in posterior part; R+M vein of hemelytron indistinct.....................................................................................................
Omoplax Horváth, 1912

- Paranotum rounded in its entire length; R+M vein of hemelytron distinct................................................................................................
Norba Horváth, 1912
(subgenus of
Stephanitis Stål, 1873
)

25 Outer margin of paranotum angular; pronotal carinae carinate..................................
Belenus Distant, 1909

- Outer margin of paranotum rounded; pronotal carinae rounded................................
Galeatus Curtis, 1833

26 Paranotum protruding posteriad; lateral carinae of pronotum rounded...............................................................................................
Menodora Horváth, 1912
(subgenus of
Stephanitis Stål, 1873
)

- Paranotum not protruding posteriad; lateral carinae of pronotum carinate...................................................................................
Stephanitis Stål, 1873
(nominotypical subgenus of
Stephanitis Stål, 1873
)
27 Discoidal area of hemelytron scraggy, raised upward at apical angle............................................ 28
- Discoidal area of hemelytron flat, not raised upward at apical angle............................................ 30

28 Hood semi-ellipsoid; ostiolar peritreme well-developed......................................
Dulinius Distant, 1903
- Hood roof-shaped; ostiolar peritreme undeveloped.......................................................... 29

29 Paranotum bulged upward; outer margin of paranotum not resting on pronotal disc in its entire length.....................................................................................................
Cochlochila Stål 1873

- Paranotum not bulged upward; outer margin of paranotum resting on pronotal disc in its entire length.....
Dictyla Stål, 1874

30 Paranotum with a longitudinal ridge dividing whole into dorsal and ventral parts; paratergite IX in female bilobed.........................................................................................
Heissiella Péricart, 1984
- Paranotum without longitudinal ridge; paratergite IX in female unilobed......................................... 31

31 Head longer than its maximum width across compound eyes; rostrum reaching abdominal sternite IV....................................................................................................
Xynotingis Drake, 1948
- Head shorter than its maximum width across compound eyes; rostrum not reaching abdominal sternite IV.............. 32
32 Outer margin of paranotum not resting on pronotal disc at least except anterior and posterior ends.................... 33
- Outer margin of paranotum resting on pronotal disc in its entire length.......................................... 34

33 Antennal segments I separated from each other at their bases; hood roof-shaped, lower than pronotal disc at highest part; outer margin of paranotum not resting on pronotal disc in its entire length.............................
Idiocysta
China , 1930

- Antennal segments I close to each other at their bases; hood semi-globose, higher than pronotal disc at highest part; outer margin of paranotum resting on pronotal disc at anterior and posterior ends.........................
Limnostatua
gen. nov.

34 Hood semi-globose or semi-ellipsoid, higher than pronotal disc at highest part; anterior margins of hemelytra nearly parallel to each other at rest..............................................................
Baeochila Drake & Poor, 1937

- Hood roof-shaped, lower than pronotal disc at highest part; anterior margins of hemelytra not parallel to each other at rest... .................................................................................................. 35

35 Maximum width across hemelytra narrower than maximum width across paranota; lateral margin of pronotum emarginate in anterior part; metasternal laminae continuous each other at posterior ends........................
Cysteochila Stål, 1873

- Maximum width across hemelytra wider than maximum width across paranota; lateral margin of pronotum not emarginate in anterior part; metasternal laminae separated from each other at posterior ends................
Physatocheila Fieber, 1844
